_comment: Hand-curated sample dossier used by the landing-page proof card, first-dossier callouts, and the workshop locked-state preview. Crafted around the canonical Hightower's Reach example the critique uses verbatim. Schema-compatible with a real generated settlement but tuned for teaching: NPC 0 carries the wall-fund corruption secret, the supply chain has a stated 11-day failure timeline, plot hook 0 is the missing-ledger arc. Update the script scripts/build-sample-dossier.mjs if regeneration is ever needed.
name: Hightower's Reach
pressureSentence: A walled border town that stopped pretending the salt-road still works.
arrivalScene: Stone walls cap a low hill above the road. Salt-cured smoke from the smokehouses drifts north on the prevailing wind. Most travellers see the garrison first, and the new wing is two seasons too new to weather honestly. Locals nod, then watch you walk past.

npcs.name: Captain Velda Marsh
npcs.secret: Skimming the wall-fund to pay for outer-watch patrols. Knows the wall will crumble inside three winters. Believes the alternative (monsters in the streets) is worse.
npcs.want: Keep the lie alive six more months.
npcs.publicFace: stern, respected, the woman who keeps the town safe

npcs.name: Brother Aldwin Vesh
npcs.secret: Has the missing wall-fund ledger. Won't hand it over until someone asks the right question; wants the crisis to break on its own terms.
npcs.want: For someone to make the choice he can't.
npcs.publicFace: patient, scholarly, never volunteers an opinion

npcs.name: Mira Thorne
npcs.secret: Has spent her own coin three times to keep convoys moving. Her name is on the wreck the moment the route closes.
npcs.want: A new route, through anywhere, before her name is on the wreck.

npcs.name: Old Han
npcs.secret: Was at the pass the night the last convoy was attacked. Knows the attacker wasn't a monster.
npcs.want: To be left alone. Will talk for wine.

failures.reason: The pass closes intermittently. Grain caravans turn back. Exports of preserved meat (which depend on the grain-fed livestock) collapse within the same window.

plotHooks.title: The missing ledger
plotHooks.body: Wall-fund records have vanished from the Sanctuary's archive. The Captain blames the merchant guilds; the merchants blame the militia; the truth is sitting in plain sight on Brother Aldwin's desk.

plotHooks.title: Salt convoy three days late
plotHooks.body: Players overhear two guildwives arguing about it at the Greyhorse. Mira pays good coin for outriders willing to ride to Whitestone Pass.

plotHooks.title: A funeral with no body
plotHooks.body: Brother Aldwin holds a vigil for a stable-hand 'found at the pass.' Old Han knows the man is alive and hiding.

events.title: Founded as a salt-road waystation
events.summary: Frontier outpost on the Whitestone trade route.

events.title: The convoy attack at Whitestone Pass
events.summary: Route closes intermittently. The wall-fund is first dipped to pay outriders.

events.title: Garrison expanded, wall-fund drained
events.summary: Captain Marsh quietly begins the shakedowns of the merchant guilds.
